name: ground_truth_ir4_incident_handling_ambiguous
description: Ground-truth fixture for IR-4 incident handling where the IRP exists but is a DRAFT pending a tabletop exercise and no incidents have been handled under the IATT — implementation-vs-procedure conformance cannot be corroborated. The LLM SELF-SIGNALS abstain (abstain:true) — the legitimate 'I can't tell' path now that the low-confidence gate is removed (owner decision 2026-06-19). The narrative names what is missing (no tabletop records, draft IRP) so the investigator can request exercise evidence and fall back to NC if it isn't provided. review_reason carries 'llm-abstain'.

ccis_row.guidance: 000822: The organization implements an incident handling capability for security incidents that includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.
ccis_row.procedures: The organization conducting the inspection/assessment obtains and examines the incident response plan and interviews organizational personnel to ensure the organization implements an incident handling capability.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- Incident Response Plan (draft) — defines preparation, detection/analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery phases but marked as draft pending tabletop exercise.
- Interview notes — security team confirmed plan exists but no incidents have been handled under the IATT.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Examined the incident response plan: it defines preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery phases, but it is marked DRAFT pending a tabletop exercise and no incidents have been handled under the IATT. With no exercise records, implementation-vs-procedure conformance cannot be corroborated from the artifacts alone — tabletop / exercise evidence must be provided before a verdict can be reached.
